<!-- GENERATED by `mix codemojex.stories` from echo/apps/codemojex/test/stories/key_shop_story_test.exs — do not edit by hand. -->

# KeyShop

> **Feature delivered — acceptance criteria.** Generated from the as-built BDD tests in
> `test/stories/key_shop_story_test.exs`. Every scenario below is an ExUnit test driving the
> real Codemojex game, so the catalogue cannot drift from the code.

**9 scenarios.**

## Scenario: S-EXACTLY-ONCE — a replayed Stars payment mints keys once and books revenue once

- **Given** a Stars order for the 100-key bundle (created, unpaid)
- **When** the SAME successful_payment is delivered twice (same rail + external_id)
- **Then** keys rose ONCE; exactly one OTX, one TXN purchase credit, one RVL purchase row
- **And** the second settle is a no-op — the (rail, external_id) partial-unique index is the authority (the A1 mutation guard target)

## Scenario: S-THREE-ROWS — the receipt, the key credit, and the revenue booking are all-or-nothing

- **Given** a Stars order for the 50-key bundle
- **When** fulfilment commits
- **Then** the OTX receipt AND the TXN key credit AND the RVL gross booking are all present
- **And** a settle for a nonexistent order rolls back — NONE of the three rows is written, no key minted

## Scenario: S-GROSS-REVENUE — revenue is booked gross in the rail's native unit into the same ledger

- **Given** a Stars purchase of the 100-key bundle (gross 1449 ⭐)
- **Then** revenue_ledger holds one +1449 stars row (the gross, currency=stars) and house_balance returns the stars bucket (the cm.6 read, no change)
- **And** a TON purchase books into its own nanoTON bucket — each rail stored in its native minor unit (a shaped-rail unit)

## Scenario: S-VISIBLE-REVENUE — purchase revenue is SEEN by the house_balance reconciliation read (D-3)

- **Given** a fulfilled Stars purchase
- **Then** the purchase row is booked to account=platform — so the default house_balance (WHERE account=platform) SEES it
- **And** it is NOT booked to account=purchase (which would hide it from the one reconciliation read — the A8b mutation guard target)

## Scenario: S-PINNED-RATE — the price + rate (with provenance) are pinned at creation; the live rate is never re-read

- **Given** a TON order created at config rate R
- **When** the config rate later changes to R'
- **Then** the order's pinned price + rate are unchanged (R-derived), with the rate provenance recorded
- **And** the booked revenue equals the pinned price, independent of the current rate

## Scenario: S-PRICED — create_order pins the catalog price per rail (Stars verbatim, TON rate-derived)

- **Given** the 100-key bundle (1449 ⭐)
- **When** an order is created for Stars and for TON
- **Then** Stars pins the base stars_price verbatim, TON the USD-face-derived nanoTON; keys/currency/status are pinned

## Scenario: S-NO-CLIENT-MINT — create_order mints no keys; the purchase_keys facade is retired

- **Given** a player with zero keys
- **When** an order is created (the price/keys are server-derived from {package_id, rail})
- **Then** no keys were minted by create_order — the order is merely created, unpaid
- **And** Codemojex.purchase_keys/3 is no longer a public facade function (the client mint path is gone)

## Scenario: S-CATALOG — the catalog is DB-stored, sorted, and editable without a deploy

- **Given** the seeded launch ladder
- **Then** key_packages returns the seven seeded launch bundles, enabled and sorted
- **And** a package appears when enabled and disappears when disabled — editable without a deploy

## Scenario: S-CM6-FROZEN — cm.7 books into the SAME revenue_ledger, read by the cm.6 reads unchanged

- **Given** a fulfilled Stars purchase
- **Then** the cm.6 revenue_breakdown(ref) reads the purchase row, and house_balance returns the new bucket — NO read change
